name: NYU Abu Dhabi
ranking_note: Ranked among top global liberal arts + research universities
citizenship_restrictions: None — all nationalities eligible
renewal_condition: Maintain good academic standing
notes: NYUAD offers a full-ride to every admitted student — covering tuition, housing, meals, flights home, and a laptop. One of the most selective and most generous programs in the world.

name: MIT
ranking_note: #1 globally (QS 2024); top-ranked in engineering, science, and technology
citizenship_restrictions: Need-blind for US citizens/PR; need-aware for international students
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated financial need
notes: MIT offers no merit scholarships — all aid is need-based and meets 100% of demonstrated need. Approximately 58% of students receive some aid. Families earning under $140k typically pay nothing. Full-ride data is not tracked separately from need-based grants.

name: Stanford University
ranking_note: #3 globally (QS 2024); elite research university in Silicon Valley
citizenship_restrictions: Need-blind for US citizens/PR; need-aware for international students
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated financial need
notes: Stanford meets 100% of demonstrated financial need for all admitted students. Families earning under $100k pay nothing; under $150k, no tuition. About 49% of students receive need-based grants. No merit scholarships exist — full-ride tracking is not applicable.

name: Harvard University
ranking_note: #4 globally (QS 2024); oldest university in the United States
citizenship_restrictions: Need-blind for US citizens/PR; need-aware for international students
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated financial need
notes: Harvard's financial aid program meets 100% of demonstrated need. Families earning under $85k pay nothing; under $150k, minimal contribution. About 53% of students receive Harvard grants averaging $53k/year. No merit scholarships — full-ride tracking is not applicable as a separate category.

name: Caltech
ranking_note: #6 globally (QS 2024); world-leading science and engineering institute
citizenship_restrictions: Need-blind for US citizens/PR; need-aware for international students
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated financial need
notes: Caltech meets 100% of demonstrated financial need for all admitted students. About 54% of undergraduates receive need-based aid. The institute is particularly welcoming of olympiad medalists, who are heavily represented in the student body. No separate merit full-ride program exists.

name: Princeton University
ranking_note: #13 globally (QS 2024); known for its no-loan financial aid policy
citizenship_restrictions: Need-blind for US citizens/PR; need-aware for international students
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated financial need
notes: Princeton's financial aid program is one of the most generous in the US — it replaces all loans with grants. Families earning under $100k pay nothing. About 53% of students receive aid averaging $61k/year. No merit scholarships — the program is entirely need-based.

name: Yale University
ranking_note: #16 globally (QS 2024); renowned for liberal arts and professional schools
citizenship_restrictions: Need-blind for US citizens/PR; need-aware for international students
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated financial need
notes: Yale meets 100% of demonstrated financial need with no loans. Families earning under $75k pay nothing. Approximately 53% of students receive financial aid averaging $60k/year. There is no separate merit scholarship program — financial aid tracking reflects need-based grants only.

name: Columbia University
ranking_note: #22 globally (QS 2024); Ivy League university in New York City
citizenship_restrictions: Need-blind for US citizens/PR; need-aware for international students
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated financial need
notes: Columbia meets 100% of demonstrated financial need for admitted US students with no loans. Families under $60k income typically pay nothing. About 53% of students receive need-based aid averaging $62k/year. No merit-based full-ride scholarships are offered.

name: University of Chicago
ranking_note: #21 globally (QS 2024); known for economics, law, and interdisciplinary research
citizenship_restrictions: Need-blind for US citizens/PR; need-aware for international students
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated financial need
notes: UChicago meets 100% of demonstrated financial need with no loans. Families earning under $60k typically pay nothing through the No Barriers program. About 55% of students receive financial aid. The Collegiate Scholars Program also supports first-generation students. No separate merit full-ride program.

name: University of Pennsylvania
ranking_note: #12 globally (QS 2024); home to Wharton School of Business
citizenship_restrictions: Need-blind for US citizens/PR; need-aware for international students
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated financial need
notes: Penn meets 100% of demonstrated financial need for admitted US students. Families earning under $65k typically pay nothing. About 46% of students receive need-based aid averaging $57k/year. Penn has no merit scholarships at the undergraduate level — all aid is need-based.

name: Duke University
ranking_note: #67 globally (QS 2024); top research university with strong premed and engineering programs
citizenship_restrictions: Robertson Scholars open to US citizens; need-based aid for all admitted US students
renewal_condition: Maintain 3.0 GPA and active participation in leadership programs (Robertson); satisfactory academic progress for need-based aid
notes: Duke's Robertson Scholars Leadership Program awards approximately 17 full-ride merit scholarships per year — covering tuition, room, board, and a leadership allowance, with exchange privileges at UNC Chapel Hill. Duke also meets 100% of demonstrated need for US students. Full-ride data reflects Robertson Scholars only.

name: Northwestern University
ranking_note: #32 globally (QS 2024); top private research university on Lake Michigan
citizenship_restrictions: Need-blind for US citizens/PR; need-aware for international students
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated financial need
notes: Northwestern meets 100% of demonstrated financial need with no required loans. Families earning under $75k typically pay nothing. About 53% of students receive need-based aid. Northwestern does not offer merit-based full-ride scholarships at the undergraduate level.

name: Cornell University
ranking_note: #13 globally (QS 2024); Ivy League with the broadest range of programs
citizenship_restrictions: Need-blind for US citizens/PR; need-aware for international students
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated financial need
notes: Cornell meets 100% of demonstrated financial need with no loans. Families earning under $75k typically pay nothing. About 53% of students receive need-based aid. Cornell hosts strong olympiad-aligned programs in chemistry and computing. No merit-based full-ride scholarships at undergraduate level.

name: Johns Hopkins University
ranking_note: #31 globally (QS 2024); #1 in research expenditure; premier biomedical research institution
citizenship_restrictions: Hodson Trust open to US citizens and permanent residents; need-based aid available to all admitted US students
renewal_condition: Maintain 3.0 GPA; demonstrate continued leadership and community engagement (Hodson); satisfactory progress for need-based aid
notes: The Hodson Trust-Gilman Scholarship at JHU awards approximately 12 full-ride merit scholarships per year to exceptional students in the sciences and humanities. JHU also meets 100% of demonstrated need for US students. The Bloomberg Distinguished Professorships further support research-focused undergraduates.

name: UC Berkeley
ranking_note: #10 globally (QS 2024); top-ranked public university in the world
citizenship_restrictions: Cal Grant and Blue and Gold Guarantee for CA residents; need-aware for out-of-state and international students
renewal_condition: Maintain 2.0 GPA (Cal Grant); demonstrate continued financial need
notes: UC Berkeley's Blue and Gold Opportunity Plan covers all tuition and fees for California residents with family income under $110k. The California Grant program provides additional aid. No formal merit-based full-ride scholarship for undergraduates — aid is primarily need-based. Berkeley's EECS and CS programs attract significant olympiad talent globally.

name: UCLA
ranking_note: #29 globally (QS 2024); most applied-to university in the United States
citizenship_restrictions: Cal Grant and UCLA Promise for CA residents; need-aware for out-of-state and international students
renewal_condition: Maintain 2.0 GPA (Cal Grant); demonstrate continued financial need
notes: UCLA's Promise covers tuition and fees for California residents with family income under $80k. The campus also awards over 4,000 scholarships annually through the UCLA Scholarship Resource Center. No universal merit-based full-ride — financial aid tracking reflects need-based grants primarily.

name: University of Oxford
ranking_note: #3 globally (THE 2024); oldest English-language university, founded c. 1096
citizenship_restrictions: UK/EU students eligible for government tuition loans; international students pay full fees (~£28,950–£37,510/year); Clarendon Fund and Reach Oxford Scholarships available
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress (Collections); good academic standing for scholarship holders
notes: Oxford offers the Reach Oxford Scholarship for international students from low-income countries and the Clarendon Fund for graduate students. At the undergraduate level, full-ride scholarships for international students are limited and highly competitive. Some individual colleges award their own bursaries. SAT range shown is for reference — Oxford strongly prefers A-levels (AAA*) or IB (38–40 points).

name: University of Cambridge
ranking_note: #2 globally (QS 2024); one of the world's oldest and most prestigious universities
citizenship_restrictions: UK students eligible for government loans; Cambridge Bursary for UK low-income students; international students pay full fees (~£24,507–£59,583/year); limited international undergraduate scholarships
renewal_condition: Maintain first-class or upper-second-class standing; satisfactory progress as determined by Director of Studies
notes: Cambridge offers the Cambridge Bursary for UK undergraduates with low household income (up to £3,500/year non-repayable). International undergraduate scholarships are extremely limited. The Gates Cambridge Scholarship is graduate-only. Cambridge strongly recruits from olympiad programs — approximately 30% of admitted physics students have olympiad recognition. A-levels (A*A*A or A*A*A*) or IB (40–42) are the standard offer.

name: ETH Zurich
ranking_note: #7 globally (QS 2024); Europe's leading science and technology university
citizenship_restrictions: Open to all nationalities; Swiss/EU students admitted via Swiss Matura; non-EU students take a competitive entrance exam (pass rate ~30%); tuition is ~CHF 730/semester regardless of nationality
scholarship_deadline: December 15 (ETH Zurich Excellence Scholarship — Master's only); Bachelor's scholarships via Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships
renewal_condition: Maintain excellent academic standing; complete required coursework on schedule
notes: ETH Zurich is effectively one of the most affordable world-class universities — tuition is CHF 730/semester (~$800) for all students regardless of nationality. Bachelor's admission for non-Swiss students requires passing a competitive entrance examination. The ETH Excellence Scholarship is graduate-level only. The Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships support some international undergrads. Full-ride tracking is not applicable given the minimal tuition.

name: Imperial College London
ranking_note: #8 globally (QS 2024); specialist science, engineering, medicine and business university
citizenship_restrictions: UK students pay regulated fees (£9,535/year); international students pay £35,100–£43,000/year; President's Undergraduate Scholarship available for top international applicants
scholarship_deadline: January 15 (UCAS); scholarship applications typically in February–March
renewal_condition: Maintain first-class performance; continue active academic engagement
notes: Imperial's President's Undergraduate Scholarship offers £10,000/year to a small cohort of outstanding international applicants, covering partial tuition. Full rides are not offered to undergraduates. Imperial is particularly strong for physics and computing olympiad participants. Full-ride tracking data is not publicly available.

name: UCL (University College London)
ranking_note: #9 globally (QS 2024); multidisciplinary research university in central London
citizenship_restrictions: UK students pay regulated fees; international students pay £21,100–£35,000/year; UCL Global Undergraduate Scholarship and Denys Holland Scholarship available
scholarship_deadline: January 25 (UCAS); UCL scholarships awarded post-admission in April–May
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress; financial need may be reassessed annually
notes: UCL offers the Denys Holland Scholarship (covers fees + maintenance) for UK students with financial need, and the UCL Global Undergraduate Scholarship (partial) for international students. Full-ride scholarships for international undergraduates are very limited. UCL's breadth of programs — from engineering to social sciences — attracts a diverse international cohort.

name: London School of Economics
ranking_note: #50 globally (QS 2024); world-leading social science institution
citizenship_restrictions: UK students pay regulated fees; international students pay £23,856–£26,208/year; LSE Undergraduate Scholarship available for UK/EU students with financial need
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress; financial need reassessed annually
notes: LSE offers the LSE Undergraduate Scholarship for UK students with financial hardship (covers fees + maintenance). International students have limited merit scholarship options. LSE is primarily relevant for social sciences, economics, law, and politics — not typical olympiad disciplines. Full-ride data for international undergraduates is not publicly tracked.

name: EPFL
ranking_note: #16 globally (QS 2024); Switzerland's leading engineering and technology institute
citizenship_restrictions: Open to all nationalities; tuition is CHF 730/semester for all students; non-Swiss students may need to demonstrate academic equivalency
scholarship_deadline: January 15 (Bachelor's admission); EPFL Excellence Fellowships for Master's level only
renewal_condition: Maintain strong academic standing; complete required coursework within standard timeframe
notes: Like ETH Zurich, EPFL charges only CHF 730/semester (~$800) regardless of nationality — making it one of the most affordable world-class universities. The famous EPFL Excellence Fellowship targets Master's students only. At undergraduate level, some need-based aid is available via Swiss social support. Olympiad medalists are strongly valued, especially in math, physics, and computer science.

name: TU Munich
ranking_note: #37 globally (QS 2024); Germany's top technical university
citizenship_restrictions: EU students pay no tuition; non-EU students pay €160.50/semester in admin fees; Deutschlandstipendium and DAAD scholarships available for international students
scholarship_deadline: January 15 (winter semester application); Deutschlandstipendium deadline varies by faculty (typically April–May)
renewal_condition: Maintain satisfactory academic progress; Deutschlandstipendium recipients must demonstrate continued merit
notes: TU Munich charges only a semester admin fee (~€161) — effectively free for all students including internationals. The Deutschlandstipendium offers €300/month (€3,600/year) to top students, funded jointly by private donors and the German government. DAAD provides additional support for international students. No formal full-ride tracking — the near-zero tuition makes cost largely a non-issue.

name: National University of Singapore
ranking_note: #8 globally (QS 2024); Asia's top-ranked university
citizenship_restrictions: ASEAN Scholarship open to ASEAN non-Singaporean nationals; Science & Technology Scholarship for international applicants; Singapore citizens pay subsidized fees
renewal_condition: Maintain minimum CAP of 3.5/5.0; maintain good conduct; reside in designated student housing
notes: NUS offers the ASEAN Undergraduate Scholarship — a full ride covering tuition, accommodation, meals, a monthly stipend, and an annual book allowance — to approximately 200–280 outstanding ASEAN nationals per year. The NUS Science & Technology Undergraduate Scholarship provides similar support for select international applicants. Olympiad medalists (especially IMO, IPhO, IOI) receive strong consideration.

name: KAIST
ranking_note: #56 globally (QS 2024); Korea's premier science and technology institute
citizenship_restrictions: All Korean undergraduates receive full tuition waiver; international students eligible for KAIST International Student Scholarship (full tuition + stipend); service bond may apply for some Korean recipients
renewal_condition: Maintain minimum GPA of 2.0/4.3; Korean students may incur tuition if GPA drops below threshold; must complete military service obligation
notes: KAIST is one of the world's most generous academic programs — the Korean government funds full tuition for all admitted Korean undergraduate students. International students are eligible for the KAIST International Student Scholarship covering tuition plus a monthly stipend (~KRW 350,000). Approximately 90% of all admitted students receive some form of tuition waiver or full scholarship. KAIST has produced numerous IMO, IPhO, and IOI gold medalists.

name: Tsinghua University
ranking_note: #25 globally (QS 2024); China's most prestigious university
citizenship_restrictions: Admission for Chinese students via national gaokao (highly competitive by province); international students apply separately via Chinese Government Scholarship or Tsinghua Scholarship; most full-ride awards are for international applicants
scholarship_deadline: February 28 (International student application via CSC scholarship)
renewal_condition: Maintain GPA of 75/100 or higher; demonstrate continued academic engagement; conduct requirements apply
notes: Tsinghua offers the Chinese Government Scholarship and Tsinghua University Scholarship to approximately 100 international undergraduate students per year — covering tuition, accommodation, and a monthly living stipend of CNY 2,500. Chinese students are admitted through gaokao scores (the most competitive scores in the country) and many receive need-based grants. Tsinghua is among the world's most dominant olympiad-producing institutions.

name: Peking University
ranking_note: #17 globally (QS 2024); China's oldest modern research university
citizenship_restrictions: Chinese students via gaokao; international students via Chinese Government Scholarship (CSC) or Peking University Scholarship; full-ride awards primarily for international students
scholarship_deadline: March 31 (CSC scholarship application for international students)
renewal_condition: Maintain GPA of 75/100 or above; good conduct record; language proficiency requirements for Chinese-taught programs
notes: Peking University offers approximately 90 full-ride scholarship places per year for international undergraduate students through the Chinese Government Scholarship and PKU's own scholarship program. Coverage includes tuition, on-campus accommodation, and CNY 2,500/month stipend. PKU is exceptionally strong in mathematics, physics, and computer science — disciplines with strong olympiad pipelines.

name: University of Toronto
ranking_note: #25 globally (QS 2024); Canada's top research university
citizenship_restrictions: Lester B. Pearson Scholarship open to international students only; domestic students eligible for University of Toronto Scholars Program and need-based OSAP
scholarship_deadline: November 7 (Lester B. Pearson Scholarship nomination deadline)
renewal_condition: Maintain minimum cGPA of 3.5/4.0; demonstrate continued leadership and community engagement
notes: The Lester B. Pearson International Scholarship is one of Canada's most prestigious awards — covering tuition, books, incidentals, and full residence for four years for approximately 37 international students per year. Nominees must be nominated by their high school. The University of Toronto Scholars Program recognizes top domestic applicants. Pearson recipients are selected for academic excellence and demonstrated impact.

name: McGill University
ranking_note: #30 globally (QS 2024); Canada's most internationally recognized university
citizenship_restrictions: Quebec residents pay lowest fees (~CAD 2,000/year); out-of-province Canadians pay ~CAD 9,000/year; international students pay ~CAD 20,000–55,000/year; limited international merit scholarships
scholarship_deadline: January 15 (Major Entrance Scholarship consideration — automatic with application)
renewal_condition: Maintain minimum CGPA of 3.5/4.0 for renewable scholarships; first-year performance determines continuation
notes: McGill offers Major Entrance Scholarships (CAD $3,000–$10,000/year) and Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program (full ride for African students with demonstrated financial need). A confirmed full-ride merit scholarship program for general international applicants is not publicly documented — full-ride data is not tracked. McGill is most affordable for Quebec residents due to provincial tuition subsidies.

name: University of Melbourne
ranking_note: #14 globally (QS 2024); Australia's leading research university
citizenship_restrictions: Chancellor's Scholarship open to all nationalities; Melbourne International Undergraduate Scholarship available for international students; domestic students eligible for government HECS-HELP loans
renewal_condition: Maintain H1 (80%+) average; demonstrate continued academic excellence and leadership
notes: The University of Melbourne Chancellor's Scholarship awards approximately 50 full scholarships per year — covering tuition fees and an annual stipend of AUD $13,000 — to exceptional students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement and leadership. The Melbourne International Undergraduate Scholarship offers partial fee remission (~25–50%) to a larger cohort of international students. Australia's world-class university experience at a fraction of Ivy League costs.
